{"results":[{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"Architecture for Server Action with cache-tag invalidation using unstable_cache + updateTag. Page (app/page.tsx) uses unstable_cache wrapped around getPosts() with tags: ['posts'] for server-side data caching. Server Action (app/actions.ts) uses updateTag('posts') (not revalidateTag) for immediate cache invalidation with read-your-own-writes semantics. No API route needed — unstable_cache provides data caching directly in the Server Component, and updateTag invalidates that cache entry from the Server Action.

Why: In Next.js 16.2.0-canary.41, revalidateTag requires a second profile argument. The recommended 'max' profile has a 5-minute stale window (stale: 300), which would serve stale data after mutations. updateTag is purpose-built for Server Actions — it immediately invalidates the tag AND marks the path as revalidated, guaranteeing no stale content on the next page load. Unlike the fetch + API route approach, unstable_cache avoids a network round-trip for data fetching while still participating in the same tag-based cache invalidation system.","domain":"project","drawer_id":"drawer_workspace_server_actions_54c1bbe9","emotions":["determ"],"entities":["ARC","SER","ACT","PAG","BPC","API","COM","WHY","FKC","NEX","THE","AND","UNL"],"field":"general","flags":["DECISION","TECHNICAL"],"importance_stars":4,"memory_kind":"evidence","room":"server-actions","route":{"confidence":0,"reason":"global search: no taxonomy keyword match","room":null,"wing":null},"similarity":0.34618568420410156,"source_file":"claude-session: Server Action with cache invalidation","topics":["architecture","server","action","cache","tag","invalidation","using","unstable","updatetag","page","app","tsx","uses","wrapped","around","getposts","tags","posts","side","data","caching","actions","ts","not","revalidatetag","immediate","read","your","own","writes","semantics","no","api","route","needed","provides","directly","in","component","invalidates","entry","from","next","js","16","2","0","canary","41","requires","a","second","profile","argument","recommended","max","has","5","minute","stale","window","300","which","would","serve","after","mutations","is","purpose","built","it","immediately","marks","path","as","revalidated","guaranteeing","content","on","load","unlike","fetch","approach","avoids","network","round","trip","fetching","while","still","participating","same","system"],"wing":"workspace"},{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"When using unstable_cache with tags in a page that has a Server Action with updateTag, the page MUST use `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` to prevent the Full Route Cache from serving stale static HTML. Without this, the page is prerendered as static (○) at build time, and after cache invalidation via updateTag, subsequent visits may still receive the prerendered HTML instead of re-rendering with fresh data.

Why: Next.js App Router's Full Route Cache caches the rendered HTML of static pages. Cache tag invalidation (via updateTag or revalidateTag) invalidates the Data Cache but NOT the Full Route Cache automatically. By setting `force-dynamic`, every request re-renders the Server Component, which then fetches data through unstable_cache (which IS properly invalidated by updateTag). This ensures the page always reflects the latest state after mutations.","domain":"project","drawer_id":"drawer_workspace_server_actions_15721b57","emotions":["determ"],"entities":["WHE","SER","ACT","MUS","FUL","ROU","CAC","HTM","WIT","WHY","NEX","APP","DAT","NOT","XDC","COM","KJC","THI"],"field":"general","flags":["CORE","TECHNICAL"],"importance_stars":3,"memory_kind":"evidence","room":"server-actions","route":{"confidence":0,"reason":"global search: no taxonomy keyword match","room":null,"wing":null},"similarity":0.23417896032333374,"source_file":"claude-session: Server Action with cache invalidation","topics":["when","using","unstable","cache","…(+22430)

{"results":[{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"Admin product catalog page uses inline Server Action ('use server' inside page component) for "Sync latest catalog" trigger. Architecture: unstable_cache wraps getAllProducts() with tags: ['products'] for fast read-heavy browsing. The Server Action calls revalidateTag('products', 'default') inline for immediate cache invalidation, then fire-and-forgets syncCatalogFromERP() so admin gets instant response while the ~3s sync updates the in-memory store in background. Page uses export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic' to ensure re-renders after cache invalidation (preventing Full Route Cache from serving stale static HTML). The "products" cache tag is used consistently across both caching and invalidation, so any view depending on the same tag gets refreshed data after sync completes. Brief staleness between sync trigger and background completion is by design for this read-heavy admin handling thousands of SKUs.

Why: Inline Server Action keeps the sync trigger co-located with the page, no separate actions file needed. Fire-and-forget gives operators instant UX feedback while the ERP/PIM sync runs. Cache-tag model means revalidation is not coupled to any single route — all views using the "products" tag see fresh data eventually. The force-dynamic export prevents stale HTML after revalidation, which is a known pitfall (confirmed in prior session drawer_workspace_server_actions_15721b57). revalidateTag('products', 'default') uses the required second profile argument per Next.js 16.2.0-canary.41 API (confirmed in prior session drawer_workspace_server_actions_0ad6eca9).

{"results":[{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"In Next.js 16.2.0-canary.41, revalidateTag(tag) now REQUIRES a second 'profile' argument (string | { expire?: number }). Calling it with only one argument shows a deprecation warning: '"revalidateTag" without the second argument is now deprecated, add second argument of "max" or use "updateTag".' The profile argument changes behavior: { expire: 0 } triggers HARD revalidation (purges cache immediately + marks path as revalidated so the next request waits for fresh data). A positive expire value triggers stale-while-revalidate (serves stale cached content while revalidating in background). The newer updateTag(tag) function (Server Actions only) provides equivalent hard revalidation without the deprecation warning.

Why: This matters because the common single-argument revalidateTag('posts') pattern from Next.js 14/15 is deprecated. Using it will produce runtime warnings AND may silently use stale-while-revalidate instead of hard revalidation, causing users to see stale data after mutations. When building for Next.js 16, always use revalidateTag(tag, { expire: 0 }) or updateTag(tag) for guaranteed fresh data.","domain":"project","drawer_id":"drawer_workspace_server_actions_0ad6eca9","emotions":["determ"],"entities":["FKC","NEX","REQ","CAL","THE","HAR","SER","ACT","WHY","THI","USI","AND","WHE"],"field":"software-engineering","flags":["DECISION","CORE","TECHNICAL"],"importance_stars":4,"memory_kind":"evidence","room":"server-actions","route":{"confidence":1,"reason":"explicit filters provided: workspace","room":null,"wing":"workspace"},"similarity":0.18769270181655884,"source_file":"mempal://drawer/drawer_workspace_server_actions_0ad6eca9","topics":["in","next","js","16","2","0","canary","41","revalidatetag","tag","now","requires","a","second","profile","argument","string","expire","number","calling","it","only","one","shows","deprecation","warning","without","is","deprecated","add","of","max","or","updatetag","changes","behavior","triggers","hard","revalidation","purges","cache","immediately","marks","path","as","revalidated","so","request","waits","fresh","data","positive","value","stale","while","revalidate","serves","cached","content","revalidating","background","newer","function","server","actions","provides","equivalent","matters","because","common","single","posts","pattern","from","14","15","using","will","produce","runtime","warnings","may","silently","instead","causing","users","to","see","after","mutations","when","building","always","guaranteed"],"wing":"workspace"},{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"Architecture for Server Action with cache-tag invalidation using unstable_cache + updateTag. Unlike the fetch + API route approach, unstable_cache avoids a network round-trip for data fetching while still participating in the same tag-based cache invalidation system.","domain":"project","drawer_id":"drawer_workspace_server_actions_54c1bbe9","emotions":["determ"],"entities":["ARC","SER","ACT","PAG","BPC","API","COM","WHY","FKC","NEX","THE","AND","UNL"],"field":"general","flags":["DECISION","TECHNICAL"],"importance_stars":4,"memory_kind":"…(+23993)

attributes.io.input: {"wing":"workspace","room":"server-actions","content":"Use `await connection()` from `next/server` (the `\"use server\"` import — not the React function) to opt into dynamic rendering for pages that use cache-tag invalidation. This replaces the older `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` pattern. The `connection()` call makes the page render dynamically per-request, preventing the Full Route Cache from serving stale static HTML after `revalidateTag` purges the Data Cache.\n\nWhy: `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` is the older Pages-Router-era convention. `connection()` is the modern, stable Next.js API that opts into dynamic rendering by awaiting a request-time condition. It signals intent more precisely: \"this page needs to wait for the actual request connection\" rather than the blanket \"force everything dynamic.\" Cache-tag invalidation via `revalidateTag` or `updateTag` only invalidates the Data Cache (backing `unstable_cache` / fetch-with-tags); the Full Route Cache still serves stale pre-rendered HTML unless the page opts into per-request rendering. Using `connection()` in the page component body solves this cleanly without an extra export.","importance":3,"memory_kind":"evidence"}
